revision.text:
[[ar:إيشيكاوا كازوماسا]][[en:Ishikawa Kazumasa]][[eo:Ishikawa Kazumasa]][[fa:ایشیکاوا کازوماسا]][[fr:Ishikawa Kazumasa]][[it:Ishikawa Kazumasa]][[ja:石川数正]][[ko:이시카와 가즈마사]][[zh:石川数正]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Ishikawa Kazumasa in Nagashino.jpg|birth_date=1533|death_date=1593|birth_place={{ill|Mikawa Province|qid=Q768158|1=Mikawa Province}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|samurai|qid=Q38142|1=Samurai}}|title={{ill|daimyo|qid=Q61982|1=Daimyo}}|spouse={{ill|Q108453407|qid=Q108453407|1=Q108453407}}|children={{ill|Ishikawa Yasukatsu|qid=Q11585796|1=Ishikawa Yasukatsu}}; {{ill|Ishikawa Yasunaga|qid=Q11585799|1=Ishikawa Yasunaga}}; {{ill|Q118747099|qid=Q118747099|1=Q118747099}}; {{ill|Q20038671|qid=Q20038671|1=Q20038671}}|father={{ill|Q109658471|qid=Q109658471|1=Q109658471}}|native_name=石川数正}} {{Short description|Samurai of the Sengoku era; major samurai ally of the Tokugawa clan}} {{family name hatnote|Ishikawa|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox office holder |order = Lord of {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|en|Matsumoto Castle|qid=Q739612|lt=Matsumoto|1=Matsumoto Castle}} |term_start = 1590 |term_end = 1609 |name = Ishikawa Kazumasa |native_name = 石川 数正 |image = Ishikawa Kazumasa in Nagashino.jpg |caption = Ishikawa Kazumasa in Nagashino |nickname = |birth_date = {{bir年|1534}} |death_date = {{death year and age|1609|1534}} |birth_place = {{ill|Mikawa Province|en|Mikawa Province|qid=Q768158|lt=Mikawa Province|1=Mikawa Province}} |death_place = |birth_name = |unit = [[File:Maru_ni_Sasa_Rindō_inverted.png|15px]] {{ill|Ishikawa clan|en|Ishikawa clan|qid=Q615039|lt=Ishikawa clan|1=Ishikawa clan}} |commands = {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|en|Matsumoto Castle|qid=Q739612|lt=Matsumoto Castle|1=Matsumoto Castle}} |allegiance = [[File:Tokugawa family crest.svg|15px]] {{ill|Matsudaira clan|en|Matsudaira clan|qid=Q1141077|lt=Matsudaira clan|1=Matsudaira clan}}
[[File:Japanese crest Imagawa Akadori.svg|15px]] [[Imagawa clan]]
[[File:Tokugawa family crest.svg|15px]] [[Tokugawa clan]]
[[File:Goshichi no kiri inverted.svg|15px]] {{ill|Toyotomi clan|en|Toyotomi clan|qid=Q1521658|lt=Toyotomi clan|1=Toyotomi clan}} |battles = {{ill|Siege of Terabe|en|Siege of Terabe|qid=Q3893140|lt=Siege of Terabe|1=Siege of Terabe}} (1558)
{{ill|Siege of Marune|en|Siege of Marune|qid=Q1964948|lt=Siege of Marune|1=Siege of Marune}} (1560)
[[:en:Siege of Kaminogo Castle|Siege of Kaminogo Castle]] (1562)
{{ill|Battle of Azukizaka (1564)|en|Battle of Azukizaka (1564)|qid=Q2762407|lt=Battle of Azukizaka (1564)|1=Battle of Azukizaka (1564)}}
{{ill|Battle of Anegawa|en|Battle of Anegawa|qid=Q964983|lt=Battle of Anegawa|1=Battle of Anegawa}} (1570)
{{ill|Battle of Mikatagahara|en|Battle of Mikatagahara|qid=Q690464|lt=Battle of Mikatagahara|1=Battle of Mikatagahara}} (1573)
{{ill|Battle of Nagashino|en|Battle of Nagashino|qid=Q428369|lt=Battle of Nagashino|1=Battle of Nagashino}} (1575)
{{ill|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|en|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|qid=Q1353633|lt=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|1=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute}} (1584) }} {{Nihongo|'''Ishikawa Kazumasa'''|石川 数正||1534–1609}} was a Japanese notable retainer under {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, who served him since childhood, when they were both hostages under the [[:en:Imagawa|Imagawa]] in 1551. ==Biography== Kazumasa, also accompanied Ieyasu in the {{ill|Siege of Terabe|en|Siege of Terabe|qid=Q3893140|lt=Siege of Terabe|1=Siege of Terabe}} 1558, and later at {{ill|Siege of Marune|en|Siege of Marune|qid=Q1964948|lt=Siege of Marune|1=Siege of Marune}} 1560. After 1560, when Ieyasu abandoned the Imagawa, Kazumasa then became a valued retainer and administrator under him. In 1562, he took part in the [[:en:Siege of Kaminogo Castle|Siege of Kaminogo Castle]], when Ieyasu managed to convince {{ill|Imagawa Ujizane|en|Imagawa Ujizane|qid=Q3100078|lt=Imagawa Ujizane|1=Imagawa Ujizane}} to release his family, Kazumasa acted as guardian of the [[:en:Imagawa|Imagawa]] {{ill|Family|en|Family|qid=Q8436|lt=Family|1=Family}}, which at the time was a very dangerous task. By 1567, the majority of daimyō forces in the Tokugawa armies were organized in two divisions, each with a separate commander. Kazumasa was placed over the forces of 13 Tokugawa daimyō-vassals and his counterpart, {{ill|Sakai Tadatsugu|en|Sakai Tadatsugu|qid=Q1132756|lt=Sakai Tadatsugu|1=Sakai Tadatsugu}}, was given command over the forces of 18 daimyō-vassals. In 1573, He participated in the {{ill|Battle of Mikatagahara|en|Battle of Mikatagahara|qid=Q690464|lt=Battle of Mikatagahara|1=Battle of Mikatagahara}}.{{cite book|last1=Turnbull|first1=Stephen|title=The Samurai Sourcebook|date=2000|publisher=Cassell & C0|location=London|isbn=1854095234|pages=222–223}} and 1575 in the {{ill|Battle of Nagashino|en|Battle of Nagashino|qid=Q428369|lt=Battle of Nagashino|1=Battle of Nagashino}}. After {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}'s victory over {{ill|Shibata Katsuie|en|Shibata Katsuie|qid=Q707587|lt=Shibata Katsuie|1=Shibata Katsuie}} in 1583, Ieyasu expressed his congratulations to Hideyoshi through Kazumasa. Later, Kazumasa and {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|en|Sakakibara Yasumasa|qid=Q1062738|lt=Sakakibara Yasumasa|1=Sakakibara Yasumasa}} accordingly issued statements attacking Hideyoshi, due to the Tokugawa's decision. Kazumasa served at the {{ill|Mount Komaki|en|Mount Komaki|qid=Q2969495|lt=Komaki headquarters|1=Mount Komaki}} during the [[:en:Komaki-Nagakute Campaign|Komaki-Nagakute Campaign]] in 1584. In 1585, Kazumasa, very dismayed by what he saw as Tokugawa's foolhardy path of resistance against {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, later he switched sides to Hideyoshi. This inconvenienced Ieyasu, who had to reconstruct his military organization and defensive policies, since Kazumasa had significant knowledge about Tokugawa organization. ==Death== After Hideyoshi's death, and the establishment of [[Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa Shogunate]], Kazumasa and his family were punished by being deprived of their fief. Kazumasa then later decided to retire and live with his son [[Ishikawa Yasunaga]], until he died in 1609. ==References== {{reflist}} {{People of the Sengoku period |state=autocollapse}} [[en:Ishikawa Kazumasa]] [[ar:إيشيكاوا كازوماسا]] [[eo:Ishikawa Kazumasa]] [[fa:ایشیکاوا کازوماسا]] [[fr:Ishikawa Kazumasa]] [[it:Ishikawa Kazumasa]] [[ja:石川数正]] [[ko:이시카와 가즈마사]] [[zh:石川数正]] {{wikidata link|Q873166}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
